examples.title: Simulate Single-Tape TM using Multi-Tape TM
examples.description: Algorithm to simulate a single-tape TM on a multi-tape TM with O(n²) time overhead
examples.code:
ALGORITHM SimulateSingleTapeOnMultiTape(input_string)
BEGIN
 // Initialize k tapes
 tape1 ← input_string + # (input marker)
 tape2 ← blank_symbols
 tape3 ← blank_symbols
 current_state ← q0
 
 WHILE current_state IS NOT accept AND current_state IS NOT reject DO
 // Read symbols from all tapes
 read_symbols ← [tape1[head1], tape2[head2], tape3[head3]]
 
 // Simulate single-tape transition on tape1
 // Use tape2 to store left context, tape3 for right context
 IF simulation_requires_left_context THEN
 move_symbol_to_tape2()
 END IF
 
 // Execute transition from encoded TM
 transition ← get_transition(current_state, read_from_tape1)
 
 // Apply transition
 write_symbol_on_tape1(transition.symbol)
 move_head_on_tape1(transition.direction)
 current_state ← transition.new_state
 
 // Adjust simulation boundaries
 adjust_tape_boundaries()
 END WHILE
 
 RETURN current_state
END
examples.explanation: The simulation uses delimiters to separate different portions of the original single tape. Each simulated step may require multiple actual steps to move context between tapes, resulting in O(n²) time complexity.

examples.title: Non-deterministic TM Simulation using Deterministic TM
examples.description: Breadth-first search to simulate NTM on a deterministic TM
examples.code:
ALGORITHM SimulateNTM(NTM_description, input_w)
BEGIN
 // Queue for BFS exploration of computation tree
 config_queue ← [initial_configuration(input_w)]
 
 WHILE config_queue IS NOT empty DO
 // Get next configuration (FIFO for BFS)
 current_config ← dequeue(config_queue)
 
 // Get current state and symbol under head
 current_state ← current_config.state
 current_symbol ← current_config.tape[current_config.head_position]
 
 // Get all possible transitions
 transitions ← get_transitions(NTM_description, current_state, current_symbol)
 
 FOR EACH transition IN transitions DO
 // Create new configuration for each possible move
 new_config ← apply_transition(current_config, transition)
 
 IF new_config.state IS accept THEN
 RETURN ACCEPT
 END IF
 
 // Add to queue for further exploration
 enqueue(config_queue, new_config)
 END FOR
 END WHILE
 
 // All paths rejected
 RETURN REJECT
END
examples.explanation: This deterministic simulation explores all possible computation paths of the NTM. It uses BFS to find any accepting path. Time complexity is exponential in worst case as it explores exponentially many paths.

examples.title: Enumeration Turing Machine
examples.description: Algorithm to enumerate all strings in a language L
examples.code:
ALGORITHM EnumerateLanguage(TM_description)
BEGIN
 n ← 1
 output_count ← 0
 
 WHILE output_count < desired_count DO
 // Try all strings of length n in lexicographic order
 FOR EACH string w OF length n DO
 // Simulate TM on w
 result ← simulate_TM(TM_description, w)
 
 IF result IS accept THEN
 OUTPUT w
 output_count ← output_count + 1
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 n ← n + 1
 END WHILE
END
examples.explanation: The enumeration TM systematically generates all possible strings (by increasing length) and tests each using the TM that recognizes the language. Strings accepted by the TM are output, thus enumerating the language.
